name: DAILY JOURNAL CORP
summary: Daily Journal Corporation publishes 10 newspapers and websites covering California and Arizona news, specializing in legal and public notice advertising. Its wholly-owned subsidiary, Journal Technologies, supplies case management software (eCourt, eProsecutor, eDefender, eSupervision) to courts, prosecutor and public defender offices, probation departments, and other justice agencies in approximately 37 states and internationally. Journal Technologies constituted approximately 80% of total revenues in fiscal year 2025.

products.name: eCourt
products.description: Browser-based case processing system for courts, configurable for all case types.

products.name: eProsecutor
products.description: Browser-based case processing system for prosecutor offices.

products.name: eDefender
products.description: Browser-based case processing system for public defender offices.

products.name: eSupervision
products.description: Browser-based case processing system formerly known as eProbation for probation departments.

products.name: eFile-it
products.description: Browser-based interface for attorneys and public to electronically file court documents.

products.name: ePay-it
products.description: Online service for payment of traffic citations by credit card, includes traffic school information.

products.name: Los Angeles Daily Journal
products.description: Daily newspaper focusing on law and its impact on society, serving lawyers and judges in Los Angeles.

products.name: San Francisco Daily Journal
products.description: Daily newspaper focusing on law and its impact on society, serving lawyers and judges in San Francisco.

products.name: Daily Appellate Report
products.description: Full text and case summaries of opinions from California and federal appellate courts, included in The Daily Journals.

products.name: Court Rules Services
products.description: Multi-volume loose-leaf sets for state and federal courts in California, updated monthly.

products.name: Judicial Profiles
products.description: Interview-based articles and biographical data for nearly all active judges in California.

partnerships.description: Journal Technologies provides hosting services through AWS GovCloud.

origin.chain: SEC.gov PEM → origin.rootz.global extraction → GitHub mirror
